headline.purpose: Section-scale identity moments such as hero and contact headings.

title.purpose: Reserved for large identity moments, not compact app chrome.

subtitle.purpose: Compact headings inside the desktop windows, sized to fit app surfaces.

body.purpose: Description copy, About copy, and contact copy. Max line 60-65ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked labels for hero and compact app metadata. Always 0.14em+ tracking and rationed across the page.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Resting card surfaces (about, skills, contact). Almost invisible; suggests material.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Hover lift on primary buttons and interactive surfaces. Used only as response to state.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Reserved for hero / floating prominent elements. Used sparingly.

motion.name: transition-base
motion.purpose: Default state-change transition for color, background, border, transform.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid Operating Teal background, used for the chief CTA in app chrome or dialogs.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary"><svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"/><polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"/><line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"/></svg>Live Demo</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { align-items: center; background: #0f766e; border: 1px solid #0f766e; border-radius: 10px; color: #fff; cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 700; gap: 0.5rem; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 0.6rem 1.05rem; transition: background 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(38% 0.07 180); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #14b8a6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Hard-edge outlined button for non-primary actions (GitHub, Resume).
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { align-items: center; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.65); border: 1px solid rgba(15,42,61,0.29); border-radius: 10px; color: #102033; cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 700; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.6rem 1.05rem; transition: background 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #fff; border-color: #102033; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Accent Status Pill
components.description: Pill with a pulsing accent dot. Use only for real semantic state such as availability.
components.css: .ds-chip-accent { align-items: center; background: color-mix(in oklch, #0f766e 14%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, #0f766e 28%, transparent); border-radius: 999px; color: #0f766e; display: inline-flex; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.74rem; font-weight: 700; gap: 0.45rem; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 0.32rem 0.8rem; } .ds-chip-dot { background: #0f766e; border-radius: 999px; flex-shrink: 0; height: 7px; width: 7px; animation: ds-pulse 2.2s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.5; transform: scale(0.8); } }

components.name: Neutral Status Pill
components.css: .ds-chip-neutral { align-items: center; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); border: 1px solid rgba(15,42,61,0.17); border-radius: 999px; color: #607083; display: inline-flex; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.74rem; font-weight: 700; gap: 0.45rem; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 0.32rem 0.8rem; }

components.name: Desktop Product Launcher
components.description: The signature project affordance. Product tiles launch hosted apps and expose source links through a context menu; mobile and reduced motion use compact Live and GitHub rows.
components.html: CareFlow
components.css: .pj-dock-item { align-items:center; background:none; border:0; color:inherit; display:flex; flex-direction:column; text-decoration:none; touch-action:none; } .pj-dock-icon { border-radius:12px; box-shadow:0 3px 10px rgba(15,42,61,0.18); display:grid; height:46px; overflow:hidden; place-items:center; width:46px; } .pj-dock-item:focus-visible .pj-dock-icon { outline:2px solid #102033; outline-offset:2px; } .pj-dock-img { display:block; height:100%; width:100%; }

components.name: Tech Pill
components.description: Compact stack or metadata tag. Reads like a specimen label.
components.css: .ds-tech-pill { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.55); border: 1px solid rgba(15,42,61,0.17); border-radius: 6px; color: #102033; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 700; padding: 0.32rem 0.62rem; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: Uppercase tracked label used above section headings.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { color: #607083; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.16em; margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase; }

narrative.overview:
This portfolio reads like a working engineer presenting three builds with care, not a marketing site. On wide, motion-allowed viewports the landing becomes a cinematic macOS-style desktop: the hero is the wallpaper, About is the one local app window, and the dock launches CareFlow, RoleFit AI, and Typeset directly. Product context menus expose the matching source repositories. Mobile and reduced-motion users get the same destinations as compact project rows.

The system rejects the AI-generated SaaS landing template that has flooded the internet by 2026 (purple gradients, four-card icon grids, hero metric tiles, Inter on white, glass cards). It also rejects the editorial-magazine reflex (italic Cormorant, ruled columns, lowercase tracked metadata) that has saturated tech brand sites. The right register is engineer-pragmatic with quiet authority and one moment of confident interactivity.
keyCharacteristics: One About window and three product launchers on a cinematic desktop.
Cinematic desktop on wide screens. The dock launches hosted products and exposes source links.
Flat fallback on mobile and reduced motion. No hidden project destinations, no motion dependence.
Product-owned identity. Launchers use real favicons and restrained supporting colors.
No invented claims. Every statement maps to a hosted product, source repository, or resume fact.

rules.name: The Product-Identity Rule
rules.body: Product launchers use their real favicons and one supporting tile color. Portfolio chrome stays within the shared neutral and teal system.

rules.name: The Color-Mix Rule
rules.body: Accent-tinted About actions derive from the window accent (--accent / --accent-soft) rather than hand-tuned one-offs.

rules.name: The No-Gradient-Text Rule
rules.body: Text is always one solid color. background-clip: text with a gradient is forbidden.

rules.name: The Single-Family Rule
rules.body: One typeface family across the whole portfolio. A serif companion is forbidden.

rules.name: The Tracking-Out-Loud Rule
rules.body: Uppercase labels always use letter-spacing 0.14em or more. Tightly-set uppercase reads as shouty.

rules.name: The Headline-Anchor Rule
rules.body: Every section has one Headline and only one. App-window headings stay compact and functional.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state.

rules.name: The No-Side-Stripe Rule
rules.body: border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent is forbidden.

rules.name: The Launcher Rule
rules.body: Project depth lives in the hosted products. The portfolio provides direct Live and Source paths on every layout.

dos: Do lead visitors to the real hosted products and source repositories.
Do commit fully on identity moments while keeping app-window headings compact.
Do derive About action tints from --accent / --accent-soft.
Do keep neutrals tinted toward the brand teal. Drafting Paper (#f5f9fc) is the right base; never use #fff or #000.
Do use ≥1.25 ratios between Headline → Title → Subtitle → Body → Label.
Do cap body line length at 60-65ch.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: skip the cinematic desktop and render direct project rows.
Do keep both Live and Source destinations available on every layout.
donts: Don't ever generate the AI-slop SaaS template described in PRODUCT.md: purple gradients, four-card icon grids, hero-metric blocks, gradient text, glass cards, Inter on white.
Don't drift into the editorial-magazine reflex (italic Cormorant or Recoleta or Fraunces, three rule-separated columns, drop caps).
Don't add a second typeface family. Manrope alone, with weight contrast doing the work.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent. Side-stripe borders are an absolute ban.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient anywhere.
Don't use decorative status dots on every nav link, list row, or badge. Acceptable only when conveying real semantic state, one per page section.
Don't invent metrics or outcomes in project copy. If it isn't in the hosted product or source repo, it isn't in the case study.
Don't apply bounce or elastic easings. Ease-out exponential curves only.
Don't add scroll-driven showcase code without a real need. The current proof surface is the desktop launcher interaction.
Don't reintroduce portfolio-only product demos that duplicate the hosted apps.
